description_long: This object is a vertically hanging scroll with a cream-colored, slightly translucent surface, featuring a stylized red-brown dragon illustration in the center. The scroll is suspended by a thin cord attached to two wooden end caps, which are cylindrical and appear to be made of a light wood. The paper or fabric of the scroll is gently curved, suggesting a flexible material, and the dragon artwork is rendered in a traditional East Asian brush style. The top and bottom rods provide structure and allow the scroll to hang flat, while the cord is looped over a small, round wooden peg. There are no visible brand markings, product names, or dispenser mechanisms on the surface.
description: A hanging scroll with a cream surface and a red-brown dragon illustration, suspended by a cord and wooden end caps.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full dragon illustration and both wooden end caps.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the rolled thickness of the scroll and the cylindrical shape of the end caps.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the dragon illustration faintly visible through the scroll material.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the rolled scroll profile and the cord attachment.
